title: Ordering at Tropical Bites
description: Food & drink vocabulary + the polite way to order. I'D LIKE, COULD I HAVE, CAN I GET — the difference between rude and right.
summary_pt_br: Comidas e bebidas + fórmulas educadas para pedir: I'd like... / Could I have... / Can I get... A diferença entre soar rude e soar educado na hora de pedir em restaurantes.

scenes.subtitle_en: Food, drinks, and the magic of 'I'd like'
scenes.subtitle_pt: Comida, bebida e a mágica do 'I'd like'

scenes.text_en:
Bia stepped up to Rafa's window.
'I WANT a taco!' she said, too fast.
Rafa raised an eyebrow. 'That's a demand, not an order.
Try again. Use I'D LIKE or COULD I HAVE.'
scenes.text_pt:
A Bia chegou na janela do Rafa.
'I WANT a taco!' disse, rápido demais.
O Rafa ergueu a sobrancelha. 'Isso é exigência, não pedido.
Tenta de novo. Usa I'D LIKE ou COULD I HAVE.'

scenes.title: Polite ordering — the three magic formulas
scenes.body_en: English has a spectrum of politeness. 'I want' = blunt / kids' register. 'I'D LIKE' = neutral-polite, good anywhere. 'COULD I HAVE' = slightly more polite, nice in restaurants. 'CAN I GET' = casual US café style. All four COMMUNICATE, but 'I'd like' and 'could I have' make you sound like an adult.
scenes.body_pt: Espectro de educação: 'I want' é para criança/ grosso. 'I'D LIKE' = neutro-educado. 'COULD I HAVE' = mais educado. 'CAN I GET' = casual (cafeteria americana). Todos funcionam, mas 'I'd like' e 'could I have' soam adultos.

examples.pt: Eu gostaria de um café, por favor.

examples.en: Could I have the bill, please?

exercise.question: Which is the most polite way to order?
exercise.explanation: 'I'd like ... please' is the textbook polite order.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'I'd like ... please' é a forma educada padrão.

exercise.question: Complete: '___ ___ have the bill, please?'
exercise.explanation: COULD I HAVE = very polite request. 'Could I have the bill'.
Four levels of politeness. Adults in restaurants: use the first two.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Quatro níveis. Adulto em restaurante: use os dois primeiros.

turns.en: I'd like a taco, please — and a juice.
turns.pt: Eu gostaria de um taco, por favor — e um suco.

turns.en: Orange, please. Could I have it cold?
turns.pt: Laranja, por favor. Poderia ser gelado?

exercise.question: Complete a polite question: 'Could you bring ___ some water, please?'
exercise.explanation: 'Bring me' — object pronoun. 'Could you bring ME some water' is the standard.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'Me' é o objeto de 'bring': 'bring me something' = traga algo para mim.
